step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{C}
**Clinical Reasoning**: The evidence explicitly states that vigabatrin causes permanent bilateral concentric visual field loss ("tunnel vision") due to toxicity affecting retinal photoreceptors and ganglion cells. This results in progressive peripheral visual field defects detectable as early as 9–11 months post-initiation, with detection peaking after 5–6 years of therapy. No other listed anticonvulsant—such as levetiracetam, phenytoin, or ethosuximide—is described in relation to visual field contraction.
